verdicts.title: Budget card "was X" badge converts the original amount to home currency, destroying the foreign figure it exists to show (no-rate currencies show the raw number with the wrong symbol)
verdicts.reasoning: Budgets.tsx:354 calls formatHome(b.originalAmount, b.originalCurrency); formatHome (currency.ts:138-140) converts from->home and formatCurrency (i18n) renders with the HOME code, so the parenthetical "was" badge shows a converted home value with the home symbol instead of the user-typed foreign amount, and no-rate currencies pass through unscaled with the wrong symbol (PDF __init__.py:697 does it right via tr.money) — but it is a cosmetic secondary badge, the canonical amount still renders correctly, no data loss.

verdicts.title: Create-budget modal shows "Budget saved" and keeps the optimistic row even when the server REJECTS the write (409 duplicate-scope / 4xx) — row then silently vanishes on next sync
verdicts.reasoning: helpers.ts:408-417 awaits upsertBudget then unconditionally close()+saved-toast inside try, but _upsertWithUpdatedAt (api.ts:417/419/435) RESOLVES {ok:false} on 409/4xx/network-error and never throws, so the catch is dead and the modal never checks result.ok — on a server rejection it falsely reports "saved", keeps the optimistic row, and (on 409) api.ts:413-416 then fires a stale-edit toast + pullFromServer that wipes the row, exactly the honest-save pattern ManualTab.tsx already applies but budgets missed.

verdicts.title: Account-wide ('all trips') budget is silently hidden and unrecoverable when the active trip is the only trip carrying budgets
verdicts.reasoning: With budgets [{tripId:'t1'},{tripId:'all'}] and active=t1, the lazy init (Budgets.tsx:46-50) sets filterTrip='t1' (the .some matches), visibleBudgets (53) exact-matches tripId so the 'all' row is excluded, and tripsInBudgets (80-81) drops 'all' leaving length 1 so showTripChips=false (83) — no reset chip renders, so the 'all' budget is hidden on this page with no UI to clear the filter; but the data is intact (still in STATE/server, still shown in Insights via tripId==='all' at Insights.tsx:477) and recovers by adding/removing any budget, so impact is a hidden card not lost data.

verdicts.title: Overall card 'over-budget' threshold uses strict `>` while individual cards use `>=` — same numbers read "Over budget" on the card but "Near limit" on the Overall
verdicts.reasoning: Budgets.tsx:72 gates Overall over on totalSpent > totalAllocated while helpers.ts:200 gates the per-card on spent >= target, so at exact equality (pct=100) a single budget reads 'Over budget' on its card but the Overall falls through to overallPct>80 -> 'near' (Budgets.tsx:74) — a real but cosmetic edge-equality inconsistency, no data impact.

verdicts.title: Read side hard-assumes budget.amount is EUR and ignores the stored currency column — a non-EUR budget from the API/CSV-import/legacy path is misread as euros
verdicts.reasoning: grep confirms b.currency is never read in Budgets.tsx/helpers.ts; amount is always passed to formatHome(..., 'EUR') (Budgets.tsx:352, helpers.ts:258, Insights.tsx:481) and budgetStatus uses budget.amount as EUR (helpers.ts:198), while the server stores amount/currency verbatim with no EUR conversion (budgets.py:48-49) and only rejects no-rate non-EUR (the IA-10 gate at budgets.py:64-71 still ACCEPTS e.g. currency:USD with a rate) — so a raw /api/budgets caller can desync amount from EUR; latent because the only first-party write path (the modal, helpers.ts:400) forces EUR and /api/sync now ignores budgets (data.py:791).

verdicts.title: Budgets page recomputes spend O(budgets × expenses) three times per render with no memoization
verdicts.reasoning: No useMemo/useCallback exists (grep empty; only useState imported), so each render runs allocatedAcrossBudgets (O(budgets^2), helpers.ts:174 .some over all budgets) + spentAcrossBudgets (O(expenses*budgets)) + overBudgetCount calling budgetStatus->spentForBudget per budget (Budgets.tsx:67) + the card map calling budgetStatus AGAIN per card (Budgets.tsx:301), and the component subscribes to expenses (line 34) so every expense add re-runs all of it — a genuine but pure-perf issue that only bites at large budget×expense counts.
